growth_records.cite: Baby et al. Inferring the Minimal Genome of Mesoplasma florum by Comparative Genomics and Transposon Mutagenesis. mSystems (2018). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00198-17. [PMC5893858]
growth_records.snippet: Unlike many other members of the class Mollicutes, M. florum shows a short doubling time of <40 min, requires no sterol for growth, and has no known pathogenic potential.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time stated as '<40 min' is a general background characterization of the species (likely citing the original 1984 isolation report), not a result newly measured in this study; exact value is unspecified beyond the '<40 min' upper bound, so doubling_time_h=0.667 (40 min) is used as an upper-bound placeholder and growth_rate_per_h=ln(2)/0.667=1.04 h^-1 as the corresponding lower-bound estimate (true mu could be higher if Td is shorter than 40 min). This paper's own culture conditions for M. florum L1 (ATCC 1161 medium, 34°C, stated in Materials and Methods: 'M. florum strains were grown at 34°C in ATCC 1161 medium') are reported but not explicitly tied back to a directly remeasured doubling time figure in this paper; low confidence given the mismatch between the background claim and this paper's own measurements (transposon essentiality data), which itself provides no numeric growth-rate re-measurement.

growth_records.cite: Matteau et al. Integrative characterization of the near-minimal bacterium Mesoplasma florum. Molecular Systems Biology (2020). DOI:10.15252/msb.20209844. [PMC7745072]
growth_records.snippet: The smallest doubling time was observed at a temperature of 34°C (38 ± 5 min) while no growth was observed at a temperature higher than 36°C.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (38±5 min = 0.633 h) directly reported from colorimetric OD560 twofold-dilution growth assay across a temperature screen (~30-38°C); 34°C gave the shortest (optimal) doubling time. mu (h^-1) = ln2/0.633 = 1.095 h^-1, calculated from the reported doubling time. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.cite: Matteau et al. Integrative characterization of the near-minimal bacterium Mesoplasma florum. Molecular Systems Biology (2020). DOI:10.15252/msb.20209844. [PMC7745072]
growth_records.snippet: Using exponential curve fitting on FCM and CFUs data, we determined a doubling time of 30.8 ± 2.9 min and 32.7 ± 0.9 min, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (30.8±2.9 min = 0.513 h) from exponential-growth curve fit to flow-cytometry (FCM) cell-concentration counts during a 24 h growth-kinetics assay at 34°C. mu (h^-1) = ln2/0.513 = 1.351 h^-1, calculated from the reported doubling time. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]

growth_records.cite: Matteau et al. Integrative characterization of the near-minimal bacterium Mesoplasma florum. Molecular Systems Biology (2020). DOI:10.15252/msb.20209844. [PMC7745072]
growth_records.snippet: Using exponential curve fitting on FCM and CFUs data, we determined a doubling time of 30.8 ± 2.9 min and 32.7 ± 0.9 min, respectively.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (32.7±0.9 min = 0.545 h) from exponential-growth curve fit to colony-forming-unit (CFU) counts during the same 24 h growth-kinetics assay at 34°C (parallel method to the FCM-based entry). mu (h^-1) = ln2/0.545 = 1.272 h^-1, calculated from the reported doubling time. [metadata completed from paper Methods: oxygen]
